purpose: A single scheme registry: for every major incentive scheme this program tracks -- launch date (Cabinet approval, PRID-cited from the PIB index), the scheme's incentive window (start/end), applicant and beneficiary counts where officially reported, and closure/wind-down/succession events. Answers 'when did each scheme start, when does it end, who applied, and which have closed' in one table.
method: Launch dates and PRIDs from the repo's PIB index (data/pib_index.sqlite, Cabinet register 2020-2026); windows from scheme guidelines as reported in PIB/ministry releases; applicant/beneficiary counts ONLY where a PIB release or Parliament answer states them (counts the government has never published are marked 'not published' -- that opacity is itself a documented finding of this program); disbursal context from the PLI report card bulletin.
headline: Of the 14 PLI-family schemes: one has completed its tenure and been succeeded (LSEM -> MPMS), one expired without a successor (Drones, 31 Mar 2024), one never started paying and lost an awardee (ACC), and one lost 14 of 58 projects to withdrawal (Specialty Steel). Applicant reporting is the weakest link: 836 approved applications exist in aggregate (PRID 2230621) but only ~half the schemes have ever had their applicant counts published individually.

pli_family_registry.applicants: 16 approved in the first mobile round (PRID 1662096); Foxconn, Dixon/Padget, Samsung PIB-credited
pli_family_registry.closure_note: Succeeded by Mobile Phone Manufacturing Scheme (MPMS), Cabinet 15 Jul 2026 (PRID 2284789)

pli_family_registry.applicants: Project counts appear only in ministry ARs; PIB names zero beneficiaries -- 'not published' at release level
pli_family_registry.closure_note: Penicillin-G production restored after ~30 years (repo's clean-win case)

pli_family_registry.applicants: 28 applicants; 7 paid Rs 157.15cr to Dec 2025 (Lok Sabha reply, PRID 2239576); earlier 18 firms paid Rs 133.95cr to FY25

pli_family_registry.window: FY2021-22 to FY2025-26 (+1y option to FY2026-27)
pli_family_registry.applicants: 42 named beneficiaries (PIB); disbursal accelerating (FY26 RE Rs 1,944cr)
Not published per-scheme in PIB; combined LSEM+ITHW disbursal Rs 15,554cr (PRID 2246089)
pli_family_registry.closure_note: 1.0's Rs 7,350cr design was replaced within 27 months -- an early quiet closure

pli_family_registry.applicants: Applicant list on DoP site; PIB reports outcomes (investment 250% of target, PRID 2247771) without a count
171 approved applications (PRID 2230621); beneficiary names only on MoFPI's own PDF, never in PIB
pli_family_registry.closure_note: No successor announced; window closes FY2026-27

pli_family_registry.scheme: PLI Solar PV Modules T1 + T2
pli_family_registry.status: ACTIVE -- zero disbursed BY DESIGN (payouts start 1y after commissioning; Lok Sabha, Mar 2026)
pli_family_registry.applicants: Not published as a count in PIB; ~30 GW commissioned per MNRE
pli_family_registry.closure_note: T1 (Rs 4,500cr) effectively folded into T2's Rs 19,500cr design

pli_family_registry.applicants: 85 selected at announcement; Round-3 list of 84 quietly became 80 -- attrition never explained
pli_family_registry.closure_note: Beneficiary-count shrinkage is undocumented in any release

pli_family_registry.applicants: 4 original awardees -> 3 (Hyundai Global Motors' 20 GWh vanished from all current DHI documents); Ola's 1 GWh of 40 allocated in 'pilot production' since Mar 2024
pli_family_registry.closure_note: 10 GWh GSSS capacity re-tendered; Parliamentary Committee demanded beneficiary-wise review

pli_family_registry.applicants: 58 Round-1 projects -- 14 WITHDREW (disclosed only in PIB, PRID 2114011); Round 1.2 MoUs signed 9 Feb 2026 (+Rs 11,887cr)
pli_family_registry.closure_note: Round-1 attrition of 24% is the largest disclosed withdrawal in the PLI family

pli_family_registry.status: ACTIVE (payouts just began: Rs 54.5cr to exactly 2 unnamed companies)
pli_family_registry.applicants: 74 Round-1/2 applicants; only 26 had commenced production at last ministry count

pli_family_registry.window: Sales years FY2023-24 to FY2027-28 (start delayed 1y)
pli_family_registry.status: ACTIVE (payouts accelerating: Rs 1,350.83cr Dec 2025 -> Rs 2,377.56cr Mar 2026)
pli_family_registry.applicants: Champion OEM + component approvals; per-round counts in MHI releases, not consolidated in PIB
23 provisional beneficiaries (Asteria, Garuda, IdeaForge...); Rs 30cr disbursed in FY2022-23 (PRID 1913565)
pli_family_registry.closure_note: The only PLI to complete its window and simply end; sector turnover rose ~7x during it

pli_family_registry.applicants: Unit-level approvals: Micron (2023), Tata fab + TSAT + CG Power (Feb 2024), Kaynes (Sep 2024), 2 more units Rs 3,900cr (May 2026, PRID 2258116)
pli_family_registry.closure_note: Design continuity, not closure -- capex-subsidy model retained across both

pli_family_registry.status: ACTIVE -- fastest approval cadence in the family
pli_family_registry.applicants: 3 tranches approved within 10 months; 22 proposals in tranche 3 alone (PRID 2210864, 2 Jan 2026)
Applications not yet opened at retrieval date

aggregate_applicant_reporting.total: 836 approved applications across the PLI programme as of 31 Dec 2025 (PRID 2230621)
aggregate_applicant_reporting.the_gap: Only ~half the schemes have EVER had per-scheme applicant counts published in a PIB release or Parliament answer; Bulk Drugs, Solar and ACC have zero beneficiary names in any PIB release. AHIDF (a livestock scheme) publishes a nightly application funnel -- the standing proof that per-applicant transparency is a choice, not a constraint.

closures_and_transitions.detail: Five-year window ended; successor MPMS approved 15 Jul 2026 (PRID 2284789) -- continuity for smartphones, India's #1 export item
Three-year window ran out; no successor. Rs 30cr of Rs 120cr disbursed; 23 beneficiaries

closures_and_transitions.event: FAME-II ended -> PM E-DRIVE (REVISED: extended, not ended)
closures_and_transitions.detail: FAME-II closed Mar 2024 (bridged by EMPS Apr-Sep 2024). CORRECTION 2026-07-20 (site-verified on pmedrive.heavyindustries.gov.in, superseding this registry's first edition which recorded E-DRIVE as ended 31 Mar 2026): PM E-DRIVE was EXTENDED segment-wise -- e-2W to 31 Jul 2026, e-3W rickshaws/carts to 31 Mar 2028; only the e-3W L5 segment closed 26 Dec 2025 (final OEM claims 25 Apr 2026). Portal live with new E-Truck and E-Bus sub-portals.
The COVID credit-guarantee line lapsed in 2023 and was revived three years later (PRID 2258114) -- a closure that un-closed
Rs 7,350cr design superseded by Rs 17,000cr within 27 months (PRID 1924766)
14 of 58 projects withdrew (PRID 2114011); Round 1.2 re-opened with MoUs 9 Feb 2026
Hyundai Global Motors (20 GWh) vanished from DHI documents; 10 GWh re-tendered under GSSS; zero disbursal to date
Announced 85 -> Round-3 84 -> current 80 beneficiaries, with no explanatory release
2021 mission reaches its stated end; NMEO-Oilseeds (Rs 10,103cr, PRID 2061646) runs to 2030-31
Regional-connectivity scheme relaunched for 10 years FY2026-27 to FY2035-36, Rs 28,840cr (PRID 2245096)

closures_and_transitions.event: Solar PLI T1 folded into T2
closures_and_transitions.detail: Rs 4,500cr Tranche-1 design absorbed into Rs 19,500cr Tranche-2 (PRID 1861127)

corrections.what: PM E-DRIVE status revised from 'ended 31 Mar 2026' to 'EXTENDED segment-wise' (e-2W -> 31 Jul 2026; e-3W R/C -> 31 Mar 2028; L5 closed 26 Dec 2025) after direct verification of the live scheme portal during the ministry-catalog sweep. The original entry relied on the scheme's announced two-year window; the portal shows the extensions.

Added from NSWS verification: IFLDP (DPIIT/leather, Rs 1,700cr, 2021-26) -- NSWS carries a formal notice of closure of its application portal, consistent with the scheme's 31 Mar 2026 sunset; a closure the ministry-side pages do not surface.

readings: The scheme lifecycle now has observable ends: LSEM is the first PLI to complete a full tenure and be succeeded; Drones is the first to simply expire. End-dates are no longer hypothetical -- Food Processing's window closes FY2026-27 with no successor announced, the next one to watch.

Attrition is reported only under duress: steel's 14 withdrawals surfaced in one PIB line; White Goods' 85->80 has never been explained; ACC's lost awardee was discovered by document comparison, not disclosure.

Applicant transparency is bimodal: schemes either publish counts in Parliament answers (MedDev: 28 applicants/7 paid) or publish nothing at all (Bulk Drugs, Solar, ACC) -- there is no middle. The registry marks 'not published' explicitly because the absence is data.

Successor design confirms the five-year instrument trend: LSEM's successor (MPMS) and Semicon 2.0 arrived in the same 15 Jul 2026 Cabinet -- both continuation-of-committed-lanes, not new production-linked lanes.
